title: AgentEvaluation
description: A structured evaluation produced by an agent for a single criterion.

confidence.description: Agent's confidence level in the evaluation.

evaluator_role.description: Role of the evaluator who produced this evaluation.

evidence.description: Concrete facts the agent observed (what was seen).

passed.description: Whether the criterion passed according to this evaluation.

reasoning.description: Why those facts lead to pass/fail (the agent's reasoning chain).

Confidence.description: Agent confidence level in an evaluation.

oneOf.description: High confidence — clear evidence supports the conclusion.
Medium confidence — evidence is suggestive but not conclusive.
Low confidence — uncertain; evaluation may need human review.

EvaluatorRole.description:
The role of the evaluator who produced a gate result.

Determines trust level and audit requirements:
- `SelfEval` ("self"): the coding agent evaluates its own work (default for v0.2).
- `Independent`: a separate agent evaluates (planned for v0.3).
- `Human`: a human performed the evaluation.

oneOf.description: The coding agent evaluates its own work.
